title: astragalus garrettii
intro: Astragalus garrettii, also known as Garrett's milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Astragalus garrettii is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant is typically harvested for its roots and leaves, which can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, allowing for various forms of herbal preparations.
